_comment: Reference payload TradingView will POST to /api/webhook/tradingview. The Pine script generates this exact shape. The receiver verifies the HMAC-SHA256 of the request body against the X-Signature header (sha256=<hex>); the inline 'secret' field is convenience for human testing and is NOT what the API checks.
_setup: 1. In TradingView, create an alert on the Alpha Bot strategy.
2. Message body: leave empty — Pine emits the JSON automatically.
4. The Pine script's 'webhookSecret' input must match WEBHOOK_SECRET in .env.
5. Real signature header must be sent separately: X-Signature: sha256=<HMAC-SHA256 of raw body>.

TradingView does NOT sign requests itself, so put a reverse-proxy (Cloudflare Worker / nginx / Lambda) in front that computes the HMAC and forwards. Or run the bot behind a private endpoint and rely on the secret-in-body match plus IP allowlisting.
